_comment: Source: Michigan MDHHS Section 9-6 Pharmacy/MCA Medication and IV Supply Requirements (Revised 4/28/2023, MDHHS Reviewed 2023). Base Michigan template — MCA-specific selections not configured.
condition: Pharmacy/MCA Medication and IV Supply Requirements
protocol_reference: Michigan Medication Protocol Section 9-6: Pharmacy/MCA Medication and IV Supply Requirements — MDHHS Approved 4/28/2023
purpose: Define pharmacy and MCA responsibilities for EMS medication and IV supply management, including medication box preparation, labeling requirements, exchange program operation, 24/7 access requirements, and discrepancy resolution. Pharmacy involvement in EMS medication supply must meet Michigan pharmacy law requirements.

sections.title: Pharmacy Roles and Responsibilities
sections.reference: Michigan Protocol 9-6: Pharmacy/MCA Requirements — Pharmacy Roles
points: Participating pharmacies must operate under a written agreement with the MCA for EMS medication supply
Pharmacies are responsible for: verifying drug identity and concentration, checking expiration dates, and sealing boxes per MCA protocol
Pharmacy staff sealing medication boxes must document: pharmacist/technician name, date sealed, and seal serial number
Pharmacies must maintain records of all EMS medication exchanges per Michigan pharmacy law requirements

sections.title: Medication Box Labeling Requirements
points: Each sealed medication box must be labeled with: contents list (drug names, concentrations, quantities), expiration date of earliest-expiring item, seal serial number, and date sealed
Label must be visible without opening the box
Controlled substance boxes must be labeled per DEA and Michigan controlled substance requirements
Boxes must be labeled to identify the MCA or agency they are prepared for when required by MCA policy

sections.title: 24/7 Exchange Access
points: MCAs must ensure 24-hour, 7-day-per-week access to medication exchange for EMS agencies within the MCA
Exchange access may be provided at MCA-designated hospital emergency departments, freestanding pharmacies with 24/7 capacity, or MCA-operated exchange locations
EMS agencies must have a designated primary and backup exchange location
Exchange location availability must be communicated to all EMS agencies and updated when changes occur

sections.title: Discrepancy Resolution
sections.reference: Michigan Protocol 9-6: Pharmacy/MCA Requirements — Discrepancy Resolution
points: Discrepancies identified at exchange: pharmacy must document, hold the discrepant box, and notify the MCA
MCA must investigate discrepancies and coordinate with the DEA and MDHHS when controlled substances are involved
Pharmacy records of exchanges are subject to MCA audit and MDHHS inspection
Pharmacies must report controlled substance discrepancies per DEA requirements independently of MCA reporting

scope_notes: All EMS provider levels — pharmacy/MCA operational protocol
Pharmacy: written MCA agreement required; must verify, date-check, and seal boxes
Box label: contents, earliest expiration, seal serial number, date sealed
24/7 exchange access: required; primary and backup locations designated
Discrepancy: document, hold box, notify MCA; controlled substance discrepancies to DEA independently
